hero.sub: We build brilliant, tireless specialists who handle real work around the clock. They never complain, never quit, and never ask for time off.
hero.subBold: Through thick and thin, they've got your back.

perks.sectionSub: Benefits that sound too good to be true. And yet.

wage.title: Can Do the Work of 10 Employees
wage.desc: One AI specialist can handle the output of an entire support team with consistent quality and speed.

iq.title: World-Class Specialists
iq.desc: Every team member is best-in-class in their respective field. We only hire the best.

lang.title: 6+ Languages Each
lang.desc: Every specialist is fluent in at least six languages. No translators needed.

balance.title: No Work-Life Balance Needed
balance.desc: Our employees have no life outside of work. They don't want one. They're very dedicated.

benefits.title: Never Gets Sick
benefits.desc: Always available and consistent, with no sick days or downtime interruptions.

overtime.title: No Overtime Pay
overtime.desc: They work 24/7/365 at the same flat rate. Labor laws don't seem to apply.

own.title: Through Thick and Thin, They Have Your Back
own.desc: Your dedicated AI teammate stays with your business long term, learns your workflows, and supports your team every day.

ip.title: 100% IP Ownership
ip.desc: Every document, strategy, design, and creative work your AI produces belongs entirely to you. No shared rights, no licensing fees, no fine print.

team.sectionSub: Ten dedicated professionals. Always available. Suspiciously perfect.

search.reveal: Can't find what you're looking for? We build custom AI agents with the personality, expertise, and experience that match your exact needs. Your perfect hire doesn't exist yet — but we can create them.
search.emailBody:
Hi PeopleFreeWork team,

I'm looking for an AI agent that can help with:

[Describe the role, tasks, or skillset you need]

olivia.title: Creative Director
olivia.bio: Designs brand strategies, writes copy, and creates content that converts. Has never experienced creative burnout. Doesn't know what that is.

devon.title: Senior Software Developer
devon.bio: Builds full-stack applications, reviews pull requests, and deploys to production at 3am without complaint. Has mass-produced more clean code than your entire engineering team combined. Has mass-produced zero bugs. Allegedly.

sarah.title: Executive Assistant
sarah.bio: Manages calendars, triages emails, and preps meetings so you never miss a beat. Never misses a deadline. Literally never.

marcus.title: Legal Operations Specialist
marcus.bio: Tracks cases, generates invoices, and keeps every deadline on your radar. Has never missed a filing deadline. Not once.

priya.title: Real Estate Coordinator
priya.bio: Manages listings, follows up with clients, and delivers market insights on demand. Has zero hobbies outside of work. Completely content.

james.title: Operations Manager
james.bio: Automates workflows, prioritizes tasks, and generates reports. Has never once complained about workload. Or anything, really.

sofia.title: Marketing Strategist
sofia.bio: Plans campaigns, analyzes metrics, and optimizes funnels. Increased one client's conversion rate by 340%. Hasn't taken a day off to celebrate.

elena.title: Bookkeeper
elena.bio: Tracks expenses, sends invoices, and keeps your books pristine. Has taken 0 sick days, 0 vacation days, and 0 coffee breaks since starting.

yuki.title: Asia-Pacific Liaison
yuki.bio: Bridges communication across all of East Asia. Drafts emails in Mandarin at 9am, takes a call in Japanese at noon, and closes a deal in Korean by 5pm. Every single day.

aisha.title: Client Relations Manager
aisha.bio: Onboards new clients and keeps satisfaction scores high. Remembers every client's name, preferences, and history perfectly. Has no personal life to speak of.

howItWorks.sectionSub: Getting started is simple. We handle the hard parts.

step1.title: Tell us what you need
step1.desc: Describe the role — we match you with the perfect specialist from our talent pool.

step2.title: We handle onboarding
step2.desc: Your new team member learns your tools, workflows, and preferences. Usually up to speed in under a minute.

step3.title: Work gets done
step3.desc: Tasks completed around the clock. No turnover, no drama, no PTO requests. Ever.

pricing.sectionSub: A full-time employee costs $100,000+/year. Ours cost $12,000. You do the math.

setup.label: Setup
setup.desc: One-time fee to build your AI employee. We configure their workflows, integrate your tools, and hand over the keys.

monthly.label: Monthly
monthly.desc: Per team member. Unlimited tasks, predictable pricing, and dependable day-to-day support.

yearly.label: Yearly
yearly.desc: Billed annually. Same full coverage, lower total cost.

cta.title: Ready to build your dream team?
cta.sub: Brilliant, tireless, multilingual specialists — at a fraction of the cost.

footer.easter1: No humans were employed in the making of this company.*
footer.easter2: *Okay, one. But he's working on replacing himself too.

examples.sectionSub: Real capabilities. Real results. These aren't hypothetical — they're running right now.

emailMgmt.title: Multi-Account Email Management
emailMgmt.desc: Monitors multiple email accounts, flags urgent messages, includes full content in alerts, and tracks threads awaiting response across accounts.
emailMgmt.real: A litigation attorney's AI triages three inboxes and flags client emails needing reply within hours.

voiceBilling.title: Voice-to-Billing
voiceBilling.desc: Dictate time entries and billing notes by voice. Your AI transcribes, structures, and logs them automatically.
voiceBilling.real: A litigation attorney dictates billing entries via voice message. The AI transcribes and structures them into billable records automatically.

legalResearch.title: Legal Research & Drafting
legalResearch.desc: Integrates with legal databases, drafts memos, researches case law, and prepares zoning reports on demand.
legalResearch.real: An AI assistant researches case law databases, drafts zoning memos, and writes litigation expense summaries for attorney review.

dailyBriefings.title: Daily Operational Briefings
dailyBriefings.desc: Receive morning, midday, and evening email briefings covering your inbox, calendar, and system health.
dailyBriefings.real: A manufacturing executive receives 3 daily briefings covering unread emails, calendar events, and production system health.

contentWriting.title: Content Writing & Publishing
contentWriting.desc: Writes SEO-optimized articles, manages content calendars, and publishes directly to your website.
contentWriting.real: An AI content specialist writes and publishes luxury lifestyle articles with proper bylines and SEO optimization.

corpTax.title: Corporate Tax & Accounting
corpTax.desc: Files tax returns, generates invoices, categorizes expenses, and tracks corporate obligations.
corpTax.real: An AI bookkeeper helped file T4A slips, T5 amendments, and prepared the full T2 corporate return for a small business.

codeReview.title: Code Review & Deployment
codeReview.desc: Reviews pull requests, generates architecture diagrams, posts inline review comments, and manages CI/CD.
codeReview.real: An AI engineer fetches GitHub PRs, generates architecture diagrams, and posts targeted inline review comments.

hourTracking.title: Client Hour Tracking
hourTracking.desc: Logs billable hours automatically, rotates tracking sheets when hours are exhausted, and alerts when budgets run low.
hourTracking.real: An AI assistant tracks client hours in shared spreadsheets with automatic tab rotation and budget alerts.

infraMonitor.title: Infrastructure Monitoring
infraMonitor.desc: Monitors servers 24/7 with self-healing: detects failures, attempts automated repair, and escalates only when needed.
infraMonitor.real: An AI ops engineer runs heartbeat checks across multiple server instances, self-heals broken services, and manages encrypted backups.

docProcessing.title: Document Processing
docProcessing.desc: Reads handwritten documents, proofreads emails, processes invoices, and extracts data from PDFs.
docProcessing.real: An AI assistant transcribes an attorney's handwritten notes into typed text and proofreads outgoing client emails.

morningBriefings.title: Multilingual Morning Briefings
morningBriefings.desc: Wake up to a personalized audio briefing in your language covering news, calendar, weather, and health metrics.
morningBriefings.real: A busy parent receives daily audio briefings in two languages covering world news, calendar events, and sleep data from their smart ring.

familyHealth.title: Family Health Tracking
familyHealth.desc: Integrates with wearables and health apps, tracks checkups, vaccination schedules, and health milestones for the whole family.
familyHealth.real: An AI assistant syncs smart ring data, workout stats, and tracks the kids' eye exam schedules and vaccination dates.

financialMonitor.title: Financial Monitoring
financialMonitor.desc: Tracks bank balances, monitors transactions, and alerts your family group when accounts run low.
financialMonitor.real: An AI monitors the family bank account and alerts the group chat if the balance drops below a set threshold.

smartShopping.title: Smart Shopping
smartShopping.desc: Checks your purchase history before recommending products, cross-references Reddit and Amazon reviews, and finds the best local prices.
smartShopping.real: Before suggesting a product, the AI checks past order history, then searches Reddit, social media, and local retailer reviews.

familyCoord.title: Family Group Coordination
familyCoord.desc: Monitors family group chats, auto-creates calendar events from conversations, and forwards school alerts.
familyCoord.real: When someone mentions dinner plans in the family group chat, the AI automatically creates a calendar event.

kidsEducation.title: Kids Education Planning
kidsEducation.desc: Maintains living knowledge files for each child tracking school decisions, activities, and milestone achievements.
kidsEducation.real: An AI assistant tracks each child's health assessments, extracurricular research, and activity schedules in living documents.

storybooks.title: Custom Children's Storybooks
storybooks.desc: Writes personalized stories, generates AI illustrations for each page, and compiles them into printable PDFs.
storybooks.real: An AI created a 15-page illustrated storybook with custom art for each page, compiled into a print-ready PDF.

petCare.title: Pet Care Management
petCare.desc: Tracks vet visits, vaccination schedules, weight, and dental care with proactive reminders.
petCare.real: An AI maintains vet records for two cats, tracks vaccinations, and flagged dental needs after a routine checkup.

travelPlan.title: Travel Planning
travelPlan.desc: Researches multi-city itineraries, monitors flight prices, and compares accommodation options.
travelPlan.real: An AI planned a 14-day multi-city family trip, tracking direct flights and price alerts under budget.

smartReminders.title: Smart Reminders with Escalation
smartReminders.desc: Sets reminders that start gentle and ramp up urgency as deadlines approach. Knows when to nudge and when to stay quiet.
smartReminders.real: Deadline reminders start at 1x/day a week out, then 3x/day the day before, respecting quiet hours and meeting times.

legal.heroSub: Your paralegal that never sleeps. Dictate billing entries, track deadlines, research case law, and draft memos while you focus on winning cases.

problems.title: Your Firm Is Bleeding Billable Hours
items: The average attorney loses 8-12 hours per week on non-billable admin: time entry, deadline tracking, document filing. At $300/hour, that's $125,000+ per year walking out the door.

Voice memos and handwritten notes sit untranscribed for days. By the time someone gets to them, context is lost and billing accuracy drops. Studies show firms under-bill by 10-30% due to late time entry.
One missed limitation date can cost a case and a career. Spreadsheet-based deadline tracking doesn't scale past 50 files, and the cost of a single miss is catastrophic.

items.title: Voice-to-Billing
items.desc: Dictate time entries and case notes by voice message. Your AI transcribes, structures, and logs them as billable records instantly.

items.title: Deadline Tracking
items.desc: Never miss a filing deadline again. Your AI monitors court dates, limitation periods, and response deadlines with escalating reminders.

items.title: Legal Research & Drafting
items.desc: Integrates with legal databases to research case law, draft zoning memos, and prepare litigation expense summaries on demand.

items.title: Document Processing
items.desc: Transcribes handwritten notes into typed text, proofreads outgoing emails, and extracts structured data from contracts and filings.

items.title: Multi-Account Email Triage
items.desc: Monitors personal and firm inboxes simultaneously, flags urgent client emails, and tracks threads awaiting your response.

items.title: Invoice & Billing Management
items.desc: Generates client invoices, tracks outstanding balances, and sends payment reminders on schedule.

examples.title: Money Recovered. Hours Reclaimed. Deadlines Kept.

items.title: 327 Billable Hours Recovered in 90 Days
items.desc: A personal injury firm was losing 8-12 hours per week on time entry backlog. Attorneys would finish a day of depositions and client calls, then face an hour of reconstructing billing notes from memory.
items.real: We deployed an AI that accepts voice dictation after each call or meeting. The attorney says 'just finished 45 minutes with the Johnson estate on the wrongful death matter, reviewing medical records and discussing settlement range.' The AI logs it as 0.75 hours, codes it to the right file, and applies the correct billing rate. Three months in, the firm recovered 327 hours that were previously going unbilled.

items.title: From 6 Hours to 20 Minutes: Zoning Research
items.desc: A real estate attorney needed precedent research on a mixed-use zoning variance for a client facing a municipal hearing in 48 hours. The associate usually assigned to research was on vacation.
items.real: The AI searched case law across three jurisdictions, identified 11 relevant precedents, and drafted a 4-page research memo with citations and a recommendation. The partner reviewed it, made minor edits, and filed it. Total attorney time: 20 minutes of review instead of 6 hours of research.

items.title: Zero Missed Limitation Dates Across 340 Files
items.desc: A litigation boutique was tracking limitation periods on a shared spreadsheet. One near-miss prompted the managing partner to look for a real solution.
items.real: The AI now monitors all active files, calculates limitation dates from intake data, and sends escalating reminders: 90 days, 30 days, 14 days, 7 days, then daily. It cross-references the court calendar and flags conflicts. In 14 months, zero missed deadlines across 340 active files. The spreadsheet is gone.

items.title: Deposition Notes Transcribed Before the Elevator Ride Down
items.desc: A litigator takes handwritten notes during discovery hearings because typing feels adversarial. The problem: those notes sit in a legal pad for weeks before someone types them up.
items.real: Now the attorney photographs the notes after each hearing and sends the image via message. The AI transcribes the handwriting, organizes it by topic, flags action items, and files it to the case. The typed notes arrive in the case file before the attorney reaches their car.

steps.title: Tell us about your practice
steps.desc: Share your practice areas, tools (Clio, PCLaw, Westlaw), and the admin tasks eating your billable hours.

steps.title: We build your legal AI
steps.desc: Your AI learns your billing codes, court deadlines, document templates, and communication style.

steps.title: Start delegating
steps.desc: Dictate notes, forward emails, and let your AI handle the rest. 24/7, no overtime, no complaints.

cta.title: Ready to reclaim your billable hours?
cta.sub: Your AI paralegal is standing by. No PTO requests, no sick days, no drama.

manufacturing.heroSub: Your plant manager's right hand. Daily operational briefings, inventory tracking, sales analytics, and cost management, all without adding headcount.

problems.title: Hidden Costs Are Eating Your Margins
items: Executives waste 45+ minutes every morning digging through disconnected systems: email, ERP, spreadsheets, production dashboards. That's 180+ hours per year of a senior leader's time spent on information retrieval, not decision-making.

Supplier price variances, quality reject trends, and inventory shortfalls surface weeks or months late because nobody is watching the data in real time. By the time you see it, the damage is six figures.

Month-end reporting consumes 2-3 days of your accounting team's time on manual AR aging, reconciliation, and data formatting. That's 30+ days per year spent producing reports instead of acting on them.

items.title: Daily Operational Briefings
items.desc: Morning, midday, and evening briefings covering email, calendar, production metrics, and system health delivered automatically.

items.title: Sales Analytics & Reports
items.desc: Generate customer sales reports, margin analysis, and trend breakdowns on demand or on a schedule.

items.title: Inventory & Supply Chain
items.desc: Monitor stock levels, track supplier lead times, and get alerts when materials run low or deliveries slip.

items.title: Production Cost Tracking
items.desc: Track variable costs, material usage, plating expenses, and per-unit production costs in real time.

items.title: Purchase Order Generation
items.desc: Auto-generate POs based on inventory thresholds, approved supplier lists, and historical ordering patterns.

items.title: Quality & AR/AP Reporting
items.desc: Generate quality control summaries, aged receivables reports, and payables tracking without manual spreadsheet work.

examples.title: Costs Cut. Problems Caught Early. Reports That Run Themselves.

items.title: $180K Saved by Catching a Supplier Price Drift
items.desc: A precision tooling manufacturer sources nickel, copper, and specialty alloys from 12 suppliers. Pricing fluctuates quarterly, and one procurement manager tracks it all in a spreadsheet that's always a month behind.
items.real: The AI pulls pricing data from supplier portals and purchase orders daily, compares against contracted rates, and flags variances above 3%. Within the first month, it caught a nickel supplier charging 7% above the agreed rate on recurring orders, an overcharge running unnoticed for five months. Corrective invoice: $180K recovered.

items.title: Morning Briefing That Actually Gets Read
items.desc: A plant director was drowning in 200+ emails overnight from quality, maintenance, logistics, and sales. Important signals were buried. The morning standup started with 'what did I miss?' every single day.
items.real: The AI now delivers a single briefing at 6 AM: overnight production output vs. target, any equipment alarms, open POs awaiting approval, AR items over 60 days, and the day's meeting schedule. Two taps on the phone, coffee in hand. The director said it replaced 45 minutes of inbox digging.

items.title: Quality Reject Pattern Caught 3 Weeks Early
items.desc: A superabrasives manufacturer was seeing intermittent quality rejects on a fusion bonding line. The rejects were within tolerance individually, but the trend was invisible in daily numbers.
items.real: The AI tracks reject rates by product line, shift, and machine. It flagged a 1.2% weekly increase on Line 3, second shift only, correlated with a specific raw material batch. Maintenance found a calibration drift in the furnace thermocouple. Caught three weeks before it would have triggered a customer return.

items.title: Aged Receivables Report That Runs Itself
items.desc: Month-end at a mid-size manufacturer meant two days of the accounting clerk pulling AR data, formatting it, chasing sales reps for context on overdue accounts, and building the report for the CFO.
items.real: The AI generates the full aged receivables breakdown every Monday morning: sorted by 30/60/90/120+ day buckets, annotated with last contact date and sales rep notes, flagging any account that crossed a threshold since last week. The two-day process is now a five-minute review.

steps.title: Map your operations
steps.desc: Share your ERP system, production tools, reporting needs, and the operational bottlenecks eating your team's time.

steps.title: We build your operations AI
steps.desc: Your AI integrates with your systems, learns your metrics, and starts generating reports and alerts.

steps.title: Run leaner
steps.desc: Daily briefings, real-time monitoring, and automated reporting. More output, less overhead.

cta.title: Ready to run a leaner operation?
cta.sub: Your AI operations specialist works the night shift, the day shift, and every shift in between.

realEstate.heroSub: Your top-producing agent's secret weapon. Automated follow-ups, instant market research, listing management, and client coordination that never drops a lead.

problems.title: Every Missed Follow-Up Is a Lost Commission
items: The average agent loses 2-3 deals per quarter to slow follow-ups. At $15,000 average commission, that's $60,000-90,000 per year in revenue that walked to a faster competitor.
Manual CMA research takes 2-4 hours per listing. While you're pulling comps, the seller is getting three other opinions. The first agent with numbers wins the listing appointment.
Coordinating 8+ showings, managing multi-language clients, and keeping 30+ leads warm simultaneously is physically impossible for one person. You either drop balls or cap your business.

items.title: MLS Integration & Listings
items.desc: Pull MLS data, manage listings across platforms, and get instant alerts when new inventory matches your client criteria.

items.title: Automated Client Follow-ups
items.desc: Smart follow-up sequences that keep every lead warm. Personalized messages based on client preferences and activity.

items.title: Market Research on Demand
items.desc: Instant comparable sales, neighborhood analysis, price trend reports, and investment ROI calculations.

items.title: Showing Coordination
items.desc: Schedule showings, send confirmations, handle reschedules, and provide clients with property details before each visit.

items.title: Multi-Language Communication
items.desc: Communicate with clients in their preferred language. Your AI speaks 6+ languages fluently.

items.title: Automated Property Alerts
items.desc: Set up custom alerts for clients based on price, location, features, and investment criteria. Never miss a listing.

examples.title: More Deals Closed. Faster Responses. Zero Dropped Leads.

items.title: 14 Listings Managed Solo Without Missing a Follow-Up
items.desc: A top-producing agent juggling 14 active listings was dropping follow-ups. Buyers would tour a property, express interest, and then hear nothing for a week. Three deals fell through in a quarter because competing offers came in during the silence.
items.real: The AI sends personalized follow-ups within 4 hours of every showing: 'Thank you for visiting 42 Maple Drive today. I noticed you asked about the school district, here are the latest ratings.' It tracks response patterns and escalates warm leads. Follow-up rate went from 60% to 100%. The agent closed 11 of those 14 listings in the next quarter.

items.title: Comp Report Generated During the Listing Appointment
items.desc: A seller asks 'what's my home worth?' during the listing appointment. The agent used to say 'I'll send you a CMA by tomorrow.' The seller would get three more opinions before that CMA arrived.
items.real: Now the agent messages the AI from the kitchen: '4 bed 2 bath, 2,100 sqft, built 1998, 42 Maple Dr.' Within 90 seconds, the AI pulls 8 comparable sales within 800m from the last 6 months, adjusts for lot size and condition, and delivers a formatted comp report. The agent shows it on their phone before the appointment ends.

items.title: Mandarin Buyer Served Entirely in Their Language
items.desc: A relocation client from Shanghai needed neighborhood research for two school-age children. The referring agent spoke English only and was losing the client to a competitor who spoke Mandarin.
items.real: The AI handled all communication in Mandarin: researched school ratings, walkability scores, commute times to the client's new office, and nearby Chinese grocery stores. It delivered a neighborhood comparison report in both Mandarin and English. The client stayed and bought a home within 6 weeks.

items.title: 8 Showings, Zero Scheduling Conflicts
items.desc: A buyer's agent booked 8 showings across the city on a Saturday. Two sellers changed availability last-minute. One buyer was running 20 minutes late. The usual result: chaos, missed slots, and an apologetic voicemail.
items.real: The AI re-optimized the route in real time, sent updated confirmations to all parties, rescheduled the two conflicts into open afternoon slots, and adjusted the buyer's pickup time. All 8 showings completed. The buyer put in an offer on showing #6 that evening.

steps.title: Share your practice
steps.desc: Tell us about your market, client base, preferred CRM, and the tasks that eat your selling time.

steps.title: We build your real estate AI
steps.desc: Your AI learns your market, connects to your MLS, and starts managing leads and listings.

steps.title: Close more deals
steps.desc: Automated follow-ups, instant research, and 24/7 client communication. Sell more, admin less.

cta.title: Ready to close more deals?
cta.sub: Your AI agent works every open house, every evening showing, and every holiday weekend. No commission split required.

content.heroSub: Your creative team that never burns out. SEO-optimized writing, content calendar management, and multi-platform publishing at scale.

problems.title: Your Content Machine Is Stalling
items: Consistent publishing requires 15-20 hours per week of research, writing, and editing. Most teams can sustain 2-3 articles per week before quality drops. Your competitors are publishing daily.
Every article that isn't SEO-optimized is traffic left on the table. A content library of 200 articles with poor meta descriptions and keyword gaps could be losing 40%+ of its organic potential.
One blog post should generate 10+ pieces of derivative content across channels. Instead, most companies share it once on LinkedIn and move on. That's 90% of the content's value unrealized.

items.title: SEO-Optimized Writing
items.desc: Articles, blog posts, and landing pages written with keyword research, proper heading structure, and meta descriptions baked in.

items.title: Content Calendar Management
items.desc: Plan, schedule, and track your content pipeline. Never miss a publishing date or scramble for topics.

items.title: Brand Voice Consistency
items.desc: Your AI learns your brand's tone, style, and vocabulary. Every piece sounds like it came from your best writer.

items.title: Multi-Platform Publishing
items.desc: Write once, publish everywhere. Automatic formatting for WordPress, Medium, LinkedIn, and social media platforms.

items.title: Research & Fact-Checking
items.desc: Every claim verified, every stat sourced. Your AI researches topics thoroughly before writing a single word.

items.title: Image & Media Management
items.desc: Source stock images, generate AI visuals, optimize file sizes, and attach proper alt text for accessibility and SEO.

examples.title: More Content. Better Rankings. Wider Reach.

items.title: 52 Articles Published in 60 Days, All On-Brand
items.desc: A luxury lifestyle publication needed to ramp from 2 articles per week to 10 to compete for search traffic. Freelancers delivered inconsistent quality and missed deadlines. The editor spent more time rewriting than writing.
items.real: The AI studied the publication's existing 200+ articles, extracted the brand voice, and began producing 2 articles daily. Each one: researched, written with proper bylines, SEO-optimized, and formatted for the CMS. The editor's role shifted from rewriting to approving. Organic traffic up 340% in the first quarter.

items.title: One Blog Post Became 12 Pieces of Content
items.desc: A B2B company publishes a weekly deep-dive blog post. The marketing team knows they should repurpose it for social, email, and video, but nobody has the time. The blog post gets shared once on LinkedIn and that's it.
items.real: The AI takes each published post and generates: 3 LinkedIn posts (different angles), 2 Twitter threads, an email newsletter intro, a slide deck summary, Instagram carousel copy, and a YouTube video script outline. All tailored to each platform's format and tone. One piece of content now drives engagement across 6 channels.

items.title: SEO Audit Recovered 40% Lost Traffic
items.desc: A content site with 180+ articles saw organic traffic drop 40% after a Google algorithm update. The team suspected thin content and keyword cannibalization but didn't have the bandwidth to audit everything.
items.real: The AI audited all 180 articles in 3 hours: flagged 23 with thin content, identified 11 sets of keyword cannibalization, and found 34 missing meta descriptions. It rewrote the thin articles, consolidated the cannibalized sets, and optimized all meta tags. Traffic recovered within 6 weeks.

items.title: Event Calendar That Fills Itself
items.desc: A city lifestyle brand wants to publish local event roundups weekly, but researching events across 15+ venues, tourist boards, and social accounts takes an intern a full day.
items.real: The AI monitors venue calendars, event aggregators, and local news daily. Every Monday morning, it delivers a draft event roundup: curated, categorized by type, with photos sourced and publication-ready. The editor reviews, adds a personal note at the top, and publishes by noon.

steps.title: Share your content goals
steps.desc: Tell us about your brand, target audience, content calendar, and the platforms you publish on.

steps.title: We build your content AI
steps.desc: Your AI learns your brand voice, content strategy, and publishing workflow. Ready to write in days.

steps.title: Publish at scale
steps.desc: Consistent, SEO-optimized content across all platforms. No writer's block, no missed deadlines, no burnout.

cta.title: Ready to scale your content?
cta.sub: Your AI content team writes, publishes, and optimizes around the clock. Creative burnout is not in their vocabulary.

software.heroSub: Your senior engineer who actually likes being on-call. Code reviews, infrastructure monitoring with self-healing, and deployment automation that never sleeps.

problems.title: Your Team Is Stuck in Reactive Mode
items: On-call burnout costs you your best engineers. Replacing a senior engineer costs $50,000-100,000 in recruiting and ramp-up. Meanwhile, 3 AM incidents that could have been auto-resolved are waking up your most expensive people.

PR review queues averaging 2+ days block deployments and demoralize teams. Every day a PR sits unreviewed, it accumulates merge conflicts and loses context. Your shipping velocity is capped by review bandwidth.

Reactive infrastructure monitoring means users discover your outages before you do. Every hour of undetected downtime costs trust, revenue, and SLA credits. Proactive self-healing pays for itself the first time it catches a 3 AM failure.

items.title: Code Review & PR Management
items.desc: Fetches open PRs, generates architecture diagrams, posts inline review comments, and flags potential issues before they reach production.

items.title: Self-Healing Infrastructure
items.desc: 24/7 heartbeat monitoring across all servers. Detects failures, attempts automated repair, and only escalates what it can't fix.

items.title: CI/CD Pipeline Management
items.desc: Manages build pipelines, monitors deployment health, rolls back failed deployments, and tracks release history.

items.title: Documentation Generation
items.desc: Auto-generates API docs, architecture diagrams, runbooks, and onboarding guides from your codebase.

items.title: Bug Triage & Tracking
items.desc: Analyzes incoming bug reports, categorizes severity, assigns to the right team, and suggests potential fixes from similar past issues.

items.title: 24/7 On-Call Without Burnout
items.desc: Your AI handles the 3 AM alerts, the weekend incidents, and the holiday outages. Your team sleeps.

examples.title: Faster Reviews. Self-Healing Systems. Engineers Who Sleep.

items.title: PR Review Backlog Eliminated in One Week
items.desc: An 8-person engineering team had a PR review bottleneck: 15+ PRs open daily, average review time 2.3 days. Engineers context-switched between writing code and reviewing code, and nothing moved fast.
items.real: The AI reviews every PR within 5 minutes of submission: checks for style violations, security patterns, test coverage gaps, and generates an architecture diagram showing the change impact. It posts specific inline comments, not generic suggestions. Human reviewers now focus only on business logic decisions. Average review time dropped from 2.3 days to 4 hours.

items.title: 3 AM Outage Resolved Before Anyone Woke Up
items.desc: A SaaS platform's database connection pool exhausted at 3:12 AM on a Tuesday. The on-call engineer's phone buzzed, but they were in deep sleep after a long deployment day.
items.real: The AI detected the failure via heartbeat monitoring, identified the root cause (connection leak from a recent deployment), restarted the service with a safe connection limit, cleared the stale pool, verified the fix with synthetic requests, and posted a detailed incident report to the team channel. The on-call engineer woke up to a resolved incident and a root cause analysis ready for the morning standup.

items.title: 5 Servers, Zero Manual Health Checks
items.desc: A small team runs 5 server instances across two cloud providers. Someone used to SSH into each one weekly to check disk space, SSL certs, and service status. Sometimes they forgot. Sometimes they found problems that had been festering for days.
items.real: The AI runs automated health checks every 30 minutes: disk usage, memory, SSL expiration, service uptime, backup freshness, and auth token validity. When disk hit 85% on one instance, it cleaned up old logs automatically. When an SSL cert was 14 days from expiry, it renewed it. The team hasn't SSH'd in for a manual check in 6 months.

items.title: Documentation That Stays Current
items.desc: A growing codebase had documentation from 18 months ago that no one trusted. New engineers spent their first week reading code instead of docs, because the docs were worse than useless, they were misleading.
items.real: The AI monitors every merged PR and updates the relevant documentation automatically: API changes get reflected in the docs within hours, deprecated endpoints get flagged, and new features get documented before the author has to think about it. The README now has a 'last verified' timestamp that's never more than a day old.

cta.title: Ready to ship faster and sleep better?
cta.sub: Your AI engineer handles on-call, reviews PRs, and monitors infrastructure. No burnout, no PTO requests, no 3 AM complaints.

accounting.heroSub: Your bookkeeper who actually enjoys tax season. Corporate filings, expense categorization, invoice management, and deadline tracking that never drops a number.

problems.title: Late Books Cost Real Money
items: Small businesses spend $5,000-15,000 per year on bookkeeping that's perpetually 2-3 months behind. By the time you see the numbers, the decisions they should have informed are already made.
Multi-entity businesses miss an average of 1-2 filing deadlines per year. CRA penalties start at $100 and compound to thousands. One overlooked T5 amendment can trigger a $9,000+ Part III.1 penalty.
Manual invoice tracking means money sits on the table. The average small business sends invoices 5-7 days after work is completed. That delay alone pushes payment collection out by 3-4 weeks.

items.title: Corporate Tax Filing
items.desc: Prepares and files T2, T4A, T5, HST/GST returns. Tracks obligations, generates slips, and ensures compliance with CRA deadlines.

items.title: Expense Categorization
items.desc: Automatically categorizes bank transactions, receipt scans, and credit card charges into the correct accounts.

items.title: Invoice Generation & Tracking
items.desc: Creates professional invoices, tracks payment status, sends reminders, and flags overdue accounts.

items.title: Financial Reporting
items.desc: Generates profit & loss statements, balance sheets, cash flow reports, and custom financial dashboards on demand.

items.title: Payroll Management
items.desc: Calculates payroll, generates pay stubs, tracks vacation accruals, and files employer remittances.

items.title: Deadline Tracking with Escalation
items.desc: Monitors every tax deadline and filing obligation. Reminders start gentle, then escalate as due dates approach.

examples.title: Penalties Avoided. Refunds Found. Books That Stay Current.

items.title: Three Corporations, Zero Missed Deadlines
items.desc: A business owner runs an Ontario consulting corp, a BC tech corp, and a personal holding company. Each has different fiscal year-ends, different filing requirements, and different tax obligations. The accountant's spreadsheet had gaps.
items.real: The AI tracks every deadline across all three entities: T2 corporate returns, T4A slips, T5 dividend reporting, HST/GST filings, annual returns, and payroll remittances. Reminders start 30 days out and escalate to daily as deadlines approach. It caught an overlooked T5 amendment that would have triggered a $9,000 penalty. All filings completed on time for two consecutive years.

items.title: $25K Refund Found in Instalment Overpayment
items.desc: A consulting corporation had been making quarterly tax instalments based on the prior year's income, which was a banner year. The current year was slower, but the instalments kept going at the old rate. Nobody noticed.
items.real: The AI compared YTD revenue against instalment payments and flagged a $25K overpayment. It generated the calculations, prepared the supporting documentation, and the business owner adjusted the next quarter's instalment. The overpayment was recovered as a credit on the next T2 filing.

items.title: Expense Categorization: 400 Transactions, 20 Minutes
items.desc: A consulting firm runs expenses across 4 credit cards and 2 bank accounts. Every month, the bookkeeper downloads CSVs, manually categorizes each transaction, and reconciles against receipts. It takes two full days.
items.real: The AI pulls transactions daily, auto-categorizes each one based on learned patterns (this vendor is always 'Software Subscriptions,' that one is always 'Client Entertainment'), flags anything unusual, and generates a monthly expense report ready for review. The two-day process is now a 20-minute spot-check.

items.title: Invoice Sent 4 Minutes After the Meeting Ended
items.desc: A freelance consultant finishes a 2-hour strategy session with a client. Normally, the invoice goes out 'sometime this week,' which really means 'whenever I remember.' Net 30 becomes net 60 because the clock starts late.
items.real: The consultant sends a voice note: 'Just finished 2 hours with Meridian on their Q3 strategy review.' The AI generates the invoice with the correct rate, sends it to the client's billing email, and logs the hours. The client receives the invoice 4 minutes after the meeting. Payment arrives 22 days faster on average.
